subject: [Pre-read] KR/JP '25 Jan Monthly Business Update Meeting Deck_250116

content.answer:
Hi all,

Sending KR and JP's Jan business update materials attached for today's
meeting.

Thank you


-- 
*Regards,*

*Joanne Lee 이조앤*
Korea Cross Border, New Channels Commercial Team
*Address: *서울특별시 강남구 테헤란로 231 센터필드WEST

Hi all,

I’m resending the email with today’s agenda attached below. Apologies for
the delay in sending it out, and thank you for your understanding.


   1. This & next meetings will focus on '25 OKR target alignment, after
   which we will report based on the targets aligned
   2. All commercial proposals for '25 are designed to achieve the same or
   better P&L% than '24*, except for KFBS proposal. KFBS by itself is a
   positive operation but has lower profitability than seller-self SLS, so
   we'll need to decide considering platform's direction on buyer experience
   benefits of shorter BWT. Relevant contents are in the deck (*The '25 P&L
   target submitted in Nov as 18.8mil USD for full year assumed same P&L% as
   '24)
   3. Handover on SLS pnl from this week (from CB SLS Team to KRJP Team)
   thus Jan actual numbers need cross-checking (Target to finalize is Feb)
   4. New P&L target for each market also need alignment with local markets
   (Target to finalize is Feb)

Thanks

content.question: This & next meetings will focus on '25 OKR target alignment, after
All commercial proposals for '25 are designed to achieve the same or
Handover on SLS pnl from this week (from CB SLS Team to KRJP Team)
New P&L target for each market also need alignment with local markets

content.answer:
it will only have 0.08-day reduction as the leadtime of subsequest
      process cannot be shortened(e.g. KR airport do not receive cargo after
      10pm, WH scan for 2nd pick up likely to be after 12am etc)  and 2) will
      incur 30~50% increase of operation cost(e.g. After hour labor
cost) of TWH
      leading to SHP cost increase



Slide 46

   -

   Does it make sense to push Video for JP?
   -

      Yes I think we can push for video in JP, need to look more in to
      details

Out of total video orders only 30% of Shopee video order is generated by
AMS-Shopee Videos, meaning 70% are seller generated videos
* However need to double check the definitions

Live stream’s ado is 50% AMS and 50% non AMS, meaning if platform
focuses more on video KOL sourcing we may also see potential by +20% ratio
for AMS Video orders


So in conclusion, I need to deep dive into JP sellers more however as the
baseline of the JP is very low, it would make more sense to push videos
rather than pushing for LS which is significantly for Mall sellers



Slide 53

   -

   Has this already been budgeted for this year?
   -

      Not yet. Feb SLS budget just submitted to CBBI team with current LH
      quotation(not the new LH quotation previously we got from 3PLs for ‘25).
      Once the ‘25 LH RFQ is resumed and awared(target date Q1), KRJP
will submit
      the budget accordingly for that month onward as from Feb KRJP logi will
      absorb SG SLS scope for KRJP



Slide 56

   -

   Do we need to make so much from JP while we are just starting? Shall we
   aim to make prices more competitive instead?
   -

      Yes we should revisit the rates, now that JPTW SLS launch date has
      been accelerated due to local team’s concern on compliance/fraud
risks from
      non-SLS channel. Current rates already had been set to be lower than
      competitors’ (mainly JP Post) but we’ll review together and
re-nego on JPVN
      rates with the 3PL. Below is reference for our last competitor rate
      analysis (TH also lower), but will update.



Slide 59

   -

   How are doing on LFF targets? Joanne Lee <joanne.lee@shopee.com>
   -

      (As of Jan 16) Updated RR at 97% after 15 Jan campaign
      -

      (As of Jan 13) Jan LFF dropped 27% MoM (RR 83%), with all markets
      regressing except VN. LFF penetration now at 23.2%, -2.4%p MoM
same period
      M-1).
      -

         SG beauty brands contributed to 37% of the decline, 23% due to
         OOS, while TH beauty brands contributed 24%, with 40% of
regression from 1
         beauty brand seller transitioning to local shop)

Apologies. Yes will send at least 1day before the meeting

Pls find answers below




Slide 5

   -

   Missing RR by so much? Is it caused by any particular market? Think all
   markets are hitting topline ADO
   -

      When including Jan 15th data (just got data today), KR ADO/ADG EoM RR
      projection is updated to 100%/95%. The previous 95%/91% ADO/ADG RR was
      projection based on actual Jan 1~14th data, and our projection tracker
      could not fully reflect Jan 1

Hi all,

sending follow ups/action items from today's meeting. Thank you

#

Topic

Action Items

PIC

Deadline

Status

1

KRJP PnL

Handover from Faye CBBI (FP&A) for topline and P&L projection logic for
KRJP

Erae, Haenle, Faye

7 Feb

Work In Progress

2

KR - New seller package

Work with Reg CB, discuss the program details for MY (SG, VN already in
progress). Expand to JP sellers

Songie, Toshi, Jason

1/30 (TBD)

Work In Progress

3

JP - Market migration feature

Plan out how JP will use the new feature that helps market migration and
share impact expected (market number)

Erae, Toshi, Jason/Shuning

1/30

Work In Progress

4

JP - TP Kick-back initiative

Add existing seller segment plan and impact

Toshi

1/30

Work In Progress

5

KR - APT

Check how CN is setting different DTS based on order creation time and
propose APT reduction options with impact

Erae

Next mtg

Work In Progress

6

JP - SLS Rate Table

Check JP seller price push down effectiveness towards SLS rate table
decrease

Haenle

7 Feb

Work In Progress

7

JP Contents

Share JP contents target

Hyungi

Next mtg

Work In Progress

8

KR - KFBS WMS

Finalize comparison and make suggestion

Erae

23 Jan

Work In Progress

9

OKR

Update the ADO ADG % to CB target

Jaeeun

23 Jan

Work In Progress

10

LFF - IB System

Coordinate with BPM on the Q1/Q2 IB Scheduling System upgrade for CN to
confirm KR inclusion and gather timeline and feature details

Joanne

Feb 7

Work In Progress

11

LFF - Blackstock KPI

Align on definition of ‘force clear’ SKUs starting from Feb force clear
batch to determine blackstock KPI

Joanne, Jason

End of Jan

Work In Progress

Regards,

for item 3, I just built a tool for sku migration. @Hongjun Li
<hongjun.li@shopee.com> can share with JP team on the usage
for item 10, I will review with @Jason Yu <jason.yu@shopee.com>  tmr on the
current design to see if need adjustment. @Ren Jiahui
<Jiahui.ren@shopee.com> pls keep teams updated.

Thanks

Thanks Shuning. Item 3 is a new one? Cos we were just discussing today
after this mtg that we tried 1-2years back a sku migration feature (pricing
based on item weight and exchange rate) which we saw many blockers then
(weight input inaccuracy) so may not be very useful, but if there's a
different one now we should revisit the usefulness @Erae Sheen
<erae.sheen@shopee.com>

2025년 1월 16일 (목) 오후 10:10, Shuning Wang <shuning.wang@shopee.com>님이 작성:

Hi Yuna, yes it is a new tool, suggest take a look again with Hongjun.
however, it is still sellers responsibility to make weight accurate, for
exchange rate, if kr, there are fields in krsc to manage by seller, if is
jp, think we need to agree with sellers in my tool. Need some new
development, can detail down with Hongjun.

In general, the tool is not a blocker, with some further development, But
the info accuracy is, which the tool can  highlight to sellers’ self check
before migration

*Wang Shuning*

Shopee | Cross Border - SG | BPM - BI - SIP

*Mobile:* +65 96310932

*Address:* Galaxy, 1 Fusionopolis Way, Singapore 138632

content.question:
Thanks.



#2 – I think we wanted to have a view of existing KR sellers and their
coverage across markets right now?

That should be a low hanging fruit that
we should engage



#3 – I was referring more to the seller centre that we are building for
MYSG/IDMY etc. Would this help JP sellers migrate their SKUs easier to
these new markets?

On deadline, can see if we get some of these more action points done
earlier?
